reason: No corpus file reaches L = cube_size_limit = 65536. Largest file is 16384 bytes (25.0% of limit). Raising cube_size_limit cannot improve BWT on this corpus — files are not block-bound, so larger blocks produce identical BWT output. u16→u32 primary_index widening would add +14 bytes overhead (+2 bytes × 7 blocks) with zero ratio gain. O(n) suffix-array (SA-IS) is a throughput prerequisite for a larger-block regime, not a present ratio lever — it does not change BWT output or compression ratio. primary_index u16 is correct for the current cube_size_limit = 65536 (L ≤ u16::MAX+1). Widening to u32 deferred until a corpus justifying cube_size_limit > 65536 exists.
on_loop_complexity: bwt_encode_codes is O(n·log n × k) comparator sort, NOT O(n²·log n). SA-IS would make it O(n) — throughput win only, not a ratio lever. Prerequisite for a future larger-block regime.

primary_index_width_analysis.required_for_larger_blocks: u32 (4 bytes/block, +2 bytes overhead per block)
primary_index_width_analysis.action: deferred — no corpus justification for raising cube_size_limit
